headline: 143-year-old grocery chain closes more locations, lays off dozens
summary: Grocery store chains regularly evaluate their portfolios to determine whether underperforming or unprofitable units need to be closed. Chains will often shut down stores when a site’s lease is set to expire. In severe cases, a company might close a location long before a lease expires if it no ...

headline: Kroger Balances Legal Risks And Drone Automation In Core Operations
summary: Kroger (NYSE:KR) is facing class action lawsuits alleging certain e commerce managers were misclassified and denied overtime pay. The Teamsters union is accusing Kroger of trying to replace union labor with third party contractors. At the same time, Kroger is rolling out Corvus One drones to automate inventory checks in its cold chain facilities. Kroger, a major U.S. grocery retailer and operator of numerous supermarket banners, sits at the intersection of food retail, e commerce, and...

headline: Huge national grocery chain closes 36 stores after overexpanding
summary: The retail apocalypse continued in 2025, as 8,100 retail stores across all sectors closed for an increase of 12% compared to 2024, according to Coresight Research, as reported by WDEF-TV. Grocery chains contributed to that apocalypse, as Albertson's eliminated 380 jobs at its corporate offices in ...

headline: Costco, Kroger  Earnings, Jobs Report: Still to Come This Week
summary: A heavy slate of economic data headlines the first week of March, culminating in the February jobs report. Investors are also weighing earnings, including from Costco and Kroger, as well as market fallout from the conflict in the Middle East.

headline: Kroger: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
summary: The results exceeded Wall Street expectations. The average estimate of eight analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was for earnings of $1.20 per share. The supermarket chain posted revenue of $34.73 billion in the period, which fell short of Street forecasts.

content.title: Kroger: Fiscal Q4 Earnings Snapshot
content.summary: The results exceeded Wall Street expectations. The average estimate of eight analysts surveyed by Zacks Investment Research was for earnings of $1.20 per share. The supermarket chain posted revenue of $34.73 billion in the period, which fell short of Street forecasts.

headline: Kroger (NYSE:KR) Reports Sales Below Analyst Estimates In Q4 CY2025 Earnings
summary: Grocery retail giant Kroger (NYSE:KR) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q4 CY2025 as sales only rose 1.2% year on year to $34.73 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.35 per share was 5.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Kroger (NYSE:KR) Reports Sales Below Analyst Estimates In Q4 CY2025 Earnings
content.summary: Grocery retail giant Kroger (NYSE:KR) missed Wall Street’s revenue expectations in Q4 CY2025 as sales only rose 1.2% year on year to $34.73 billion. Its GAAP profit of $1.35 per share was 5.7% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Ciena stock slides, Trade Desk jumps on OpenAI ad talks
summary: Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre joins Markets Catalysts Host Julie Hyman to track several of the day's top trending stock tickers. Ciena (CIEN) stock drops despite reporting strong earnings, as investors react to guidance after a big run-up in the stock. Trade Desk (TTD) jumps on reports it may work with OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) to sell ads on ChatGPT. Kroger (KR) is also in focus after giving a softer profit outlook amid rising grocery competition. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

Trade Desk (TTD) jumps on reports it may work with OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) to sell ads on ChatGPT. Kroger (KR) is also in focus after giving a softer profit outlook amid rising grocery competition.

headline: Kroger’s New Boss Aims For Lower Prices in Stores
summary: The one thing Kroger’s new CEO wants to see across grocery-store aisles: lower prices.  Greg Foran, a longtime Walmart executive who took over as CEO of Kroger last month, said the Cincinnati-based supermarket giant has room to cut costs across its operations and use the savings to lower prices.  Kroger is facing rising competition from Foran’s former employer, Walmart, discount grocers such as Aldi, and other supermarket chains, including Publix.

content.summary: The one thing Kroger’s new CEO wants to see across grocery-store aisles: lower prices. Kroger is facing rising competition from Foran’s former employer, Walmart, discount grocers such as Aldi, and other supermarket chains, including Publix.

headline: Kroger Q4 Earnings Top Estimates, E-Commerce Sales Rise 20%

headline: New Kroger CEO wants to accelerate turnaround
summary: The company will focus on improving the store experience and cutting prices as it strives to grow faster, said Greg Foran, who recently took over as the grocer’s chief executive.
